name: Shako
author: Jean-Louis Cazaux (1990) / AGP port
description: Shako (Jean-Louis Cazaux, 1990), a 10x10 chess variant. The full FIDE army plus two extra piece types per side: the Cannon (the Xiangqi cannon — moves like a rook along empty lines, but captures only by jumping over exactly one screen of either colour onto the enemy beyond) and the Elephant (the medieval Ferz+Alfil — moves one or two squares diagonally, leaping the intermediate square). Cannons sit in the rank-1 corners, elephants in the rank-2 corners; the rest of the back rank is E R N B Q K B N R E with the queen on e and king on f. Pawns start on the third rank with a double-step and en passant, and promote on the far rank to Queen, Rook, Bishop, Knight, Cannon or Elephant. Castling follows orthodox rules with the f-file king. Checkmate wins; stalemate, the fifty-move rule, threefold repetition and a hard ply cap draw.
